channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Artist David Hockney said the drive to create pictures 'is deep within us'
item.description: Legendary painter David Hockney died on Thursday at the age of 88. The late NPR correspondent Susan Stamberg spoke with the artist in 2016 about his lifelong obsession with looking.
item.content:encoded: Legendary painter David Hockney died on Thursday at the age of 88. The late NPR correspondent Susan Stamberg spoke with the artist in 2016 about his lifelong obsession with looking.
item.dc:creator: Susan Stamberg

item.title: Paramount-Warner Brothers merger gets Justice Department approval
item.description: The Justice Department closed its investigation into the proposed $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Brothers Discovery, saying it found no threat to competition or consumers.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department closed its investigation into the proposed $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Brothers Discovery, saying it found no threat to competition or consumers.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Bebe Rexha's 'Dirty Blonde'
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with pop artist Bebe Rexha about her new record, Dirty Blonde.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with pop artist Bebe Rexha about her new record, Dirty Blonde.
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers

item.title: Meet a 93-year-old member of the Tartan Army of Scotland soccer fans
item.description: It's Scotland's first World Cup in nearly 30 years. Moira Brown, perhaps the oldest of Scotland's Tartan Army of soccer fans, will be in Boston when Scotland's team plays against Haiti on June 13.
item.content:encoded: It's Scotland's first World Cup in nearly 30 years. Moira Brown, perhaps the oldest of Scotland's Tartan Army of soccer fans, will be in Boston when Scotland's team plays against Haiti on June 13.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: A judge clears the way for Trump's name to be removed from the Kennedy Center
item.description: On Friday, a federal judge denied an appeal by the Kennedy Center's board of trustees for a stay on the removal of President Trump's name from the building. Then the Trump administration filed for an emergency stay, and that too was denied.
item.content:encoded: On Friday, a federal judge denied an appeal by the Kennedy Center's board of trustees for a stay on the removal of President Trump's name from the building. Then the Trump administration filed for an emergency stay, and that too was denied.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: San Antonio fans hold on to hope that the Spurs can still win the NBA Championship
item.description: San Antonio Spurs fans are looking for their rabbit foot and other good luck charms as the Spurs face a do-or-die finals game on Saturday night against the New York Knicks.
item.content:encoded: San Antonio Spurs fans are looking for their rabbit foot and other good luck charms as the Spurs face a do-or-die finals game on Saturday night against the New York Knicks.
item.dc:creator: Brian Kirkpatrick

item.title: Actor Brett Goldstein on how empathy plays into his work
item.description: On Wild Card, famous guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Actor Brett Goldstein talks about the ways he's softened as he's grown older.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, famous guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Actor Brett Goldstein talks about the ways he's softened as he's grown older.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: The U.S. Men's National Team's moment is here
item.description: For the U.S. men's national soccer team, this day has been circled on the calendar for almost a decade: The most talented generation in team history is in its prime for a FIFA World Cup on home soil. Can they pull off the unthinkable?
item.content:encoded: For the U.S. men's national soccer team, this day has been circled on the calendar for almost a decade: The most talented generation in team history is in its prime for a FIFA World Cup on home soil. Can they pull off the unthinkable?
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Ebola response is shaped by conflict in Democratic Republic of Congo
item.description: The Ebola outbreak is taking place in a region that has been through decades of deadly conflict. That's affecting how responders are doing their work, from testing to treating patients.
item.content:encoded: The Ebola outbreak is taking place in a region that has been through decades of deadly conflict. That's affecting how responders are doing their work, from testing to treating patients.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Controversy swirls around the UFC event on the White House lawn this weekend
item.description: President Trump is hosting a UFC fight on the White House lawn on his birthday.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is hosting a UFC fight on the White House lawn on his birthday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben
